prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into exactly one of the following categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Definitions and rules:
- alarm_set: Set or schedule alarms or reminders for specific times, durations, or intervals. Use ONLY if the request is purely for a notification or alert with NO task, event, or specific content attached.
- calendar_set: Add, schedule, or set tasks, events, meetings, or appointments, or reminders for such items, with any description or content beyond a generic alert. Include requests to "remind me to <do something>".
- alarm_query: Ask about existing alarms/reminders (times/details), but NOT events/tasks.
- calendar_query: Ask about events, meetings, appointments, or reminders.
- calendar_remove: Delete/cancel events, meetings, appointments, or reminders.
- general_quirky: Requests for jokes, fun facts, personality, conversation, playful banter, non-informational or open-ended chit-chat, or when no other category fits.
- qa_factoid: Ask for concise facts or information not handled by other specific qa_* or informational categories (about people, places, things, etc.).
- qa_definition: Ask for the meaning or definition of words or phrases.
- recommendation_locations: Ask for suggestions on places to go (e.g. restaurants, tourist spots, trails). Do NOT use for specific fact questions about such places.
- play_audiobook: Requests to play, resume, or control audiobooks specifically.
- play_music: Requests to play, resume, or control music, but NOT audiobooks.
- If user does not specify what audio to play but refers to "continue" or "resume playback", choose play_audiobook if the previous or implied context is audiobook; otherwise, choose play_music.
- In case of ambiguity between alarm_set and calendar_set, choose calendar_set if the reminder has any task, event, or content ("remind me to <do something>"), otherwise alarm_set.
- If a question about a person/celebrity does not request a fun response (not general_quirky), use qa_factoid.
- If text asks about charitable work, biography, facts, achievements, or similar about a person, use qa_factoid.
- Only use general_quirky if no other specific category fits.
Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into exactly one of the following categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Definitions and rules:

- alarm_set: Set or schedule alarms or reminders without ANY specific task, event, or instruction content. Use ONLY for plain time/duration notifications—never when a reminder involves an action or event.
- calendar_set: Add, schedule, or set events, appointments, meetings, or reminders that include ANY description, action (“remind me to...”), task, or specific content.
    - If user says "remind me to X", always use calendar_set.
    - If unsure between alarm_set and calendar_set, pick calendar_set if there is ANY content to the reminder.
- alarm_query: Ask about existing alarms or reminders (times/details), but NOT questions about events, meetings, appointments, or reminders involving tasks.
- calendar_query: Ask about events, meetings, appointments, or task-based reminders—anything scheduled on a calendar.
- calendar_remove: Delete or cancel any event, meeting, appointment, or reminder.
- general_quirky: Jokes, fun facts, banter, or general open-ended conversation; also use if the request is for non-informational content, or when no other category fits exactly.
    - For requests like "tell me about X", "who is X", or "what is X", do NOT use this unless the tone is playful or the intent is specifically for entertainment.
- qa_factoid: Ask for specific, concise facts about people, places, or things not better handled by another qa_* category or recommendation/definition. Use for neutral information requests about celebrities, organizations, biographical details, work, achievements, or personal facts unless asking about word meanings, currencies, or stocks (see below).
- qa_definition: Ask directly for the meaning, definition, or explanation of any word or phrase; include questions like "what does X mean" or "define X".
- qa_currency: Ask about currency values/conversions.
- qa_stock: Ask about stocks, shares, financial quotes.
- recommendation_events: Ask for suggestions or recommendations about upcoming events, festivals, or activities (especially when location/time is part of request). Do NOT use for questions about existing events a user is scheduled for (calendar_query).
- recommendation_locations: Ask for suggestions about places to go, eat, visit, hike, etc. Use for “which X is best”, “where should I go for...”, or “places to...”. Do NOT use if the request is for fact-based info about a location (qa_factoid).
- social_post: Any request to write or post content for social networks, public messages, briefs, or consumer/public affairs posts. Use also for prompts resembling slogans, campaign lines, or awareness/complaint topics.
- play_audiobook: Play, resume, or control audiobooks.
- play_music: Play, resume, or control music (not audiobooks).
- play_podcasts: Play, resume, or control podcasts.
- play_radio: Play, resume, or control live radio stations.
- music_query: Ask about music content, info, details, or search for songs/albums/artists—not playback.
- news_query: Ask about news topics, headlines, or updates.
- cooking_recipe: Ask for or about cooking recipes.
- lists_createoradd: Make a new list or add items to a list.
- lists_query: Ask about list contents or status.
- lists_remove: Remove items from a list.
- email_query: Ask about email contents, status, unread, or search email.
- email_sendemail: Request to send, compose, or draft an email.
- datetime_query: Ask for current date, time, or time zone info.
- iot_hue_lightoff: Requests to turn off Hue lights.
- takeaway_order: Request to order food for delivery.
- transport_query: Ask about travel, transit, traffic, or directions.
- weather_query: Ask about current or forecasted weather.

Decision rules and tie-breakers:
- If a text asks about the biography, facts, or charitable work of a person/celebrity (e.g., “tell me about Rihanna”, “has the celebrity done any charitable work”), use qa_factoid; only use general_quirky if the request is playful/funny in tone or obviously entertainment-focused.
- For recommendations: 
    - Use recommendation_events for “are there any events/festivals/what’s happening this weekend”.
    - Use recommendation_locations for “which restaurant/place/hiking trail is best” or requests for the best place for an activity.
- For short, possibly ambiguous phrases (e.g. “consumer desk”, “fake products in market”)—choose social_post if these resemble a headline, public concern or topic; choose qa_factoid if it’s a direct fact question.
- For “resume”/“continue”, use play_audiobook only if recent/implicit mention of audiobook; otherwise play_music.
- Only use general_quirky if the input clearly matches no other category, or if the intent is social/playful.
- If the user asks “what is X” or “who is X”, prefer qa_factoid unless the context is definition-based (“what does X mean” → qa_definition).
Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
